skills.definition: Some train to fight despite pain—others train to focus because of it. This skill transforms agony into mental clarity, sharpening awareness in the presence of discomfort.

skills.name: Mind Over Body
skills.definition: The pain is there—but it is not in charge. This skill reflects total mental dominance over physical reaction, allowing precise action even when wounded or exhausted.

skills.name: Steady Aim Under Pain
skills.definition: Whether bleeding or burning, this skill holds the hand steady. It reflects the ability to maintain precise physical control—especially for ranged or delicate actions—under active pain.

skills.name: Controlled Breathing
skills.definition: Inhale. Hold. Exhale. Controlled Breathing is a tactical response to pain, regulating oxygen, heart rate, and panic to keep focus sharp even when endurance wears thin.

skills.name: Adrenalyn Surge
skills.definition: This skill taps into the body’s primal response—flooding it with adrenaline at will. The result is a temporary surge in energy, speed, or strength at the cost of later recovery.

skills.name: Safe Spike
skills.definition: Adrenaline is a weapon, but it must be wielded with care. This skill refines the body’s spike response, increasing control and reducing the risk of cardiovascular collapse.

skills.name: Surge Sustain
skills.definition: Where others burn out in moments, this skill draws out the rush. Surge Sustain prolongs the beneficial effects of adrenaline, keeping the body in peak state for critical windows of action.

skills.name: Rapid Crash Recovery
skills.definition: When the surge ends, the crash comes fast—unless trained otherwise. This skill speeds the body’s recovery from hormonal spikes, restoring balance and preventing collapse after the storm.

skills.name: Metabolic Control
skills.definition: This foundational skill governs the body's ability to manage energy, oxygen, and temperature under stress. It reflects internal mastery over processes usually left to instinct. Through discipline and conditioning, the body becomes its own command center.

skills.name: Breath Control
skills.definition: This skill trains precise regulation of the breath to calm nerves, stabilize heart rate, and enhance oxygen efficiency. Whether meditative or combative, control begins with the inhale.

skills.name: Box Breathing
skills.definition: Used by warriors, divers, and monks alike, Box Breathing slows the mind through a measured rhythm of inhale, hold, exhale, hold. It creates stillness under pressure and balance under strain.

skills.name: CO₂ Tolerance
skills.definition: This skill enhances the body's ability to withstand elevated carbon dioxide levels. Useful in breath-hold training or oxygen-poor environments, it reduces the urge to panic and sharpens resilience.

skills.name: Oxygen Flush
skills.definition: Oxygen becomes a weaponized resource. This skill trains deep, deliberate breaths to rapidly saturate the bloodstream, flush toxins, and prepare for intense bursts of effort or endurance.

skills.name: Nutrition Cycling
skills.definition: The body learns to shift between fuel sources with tactical intention. This skill represents trained rhythms of intake and fasting, maximizing energy retention and performance across varied conditions.

skills.name: Carb Load
skills.definition: Carbohydrates become fuel for sustained bursts of action. This skill allows the body to efficiently store and draw on carbs when rapid energy output is needed, from sprinting to combat.

skills.name: Ketogenic Switch
skills.definition: The body adapts to burn fat instead of sugar for energy. This skill enables extended endurance with less food, supporting clarity and performance over long durations.

skills.name: Glycogen Sparing
skills.definition: By optimizing energy use, this skill reduces how quickly stored sugars are depleted. It extends stamina and delays exhaustion when energy is needed most.

skills.name: Thermoregulation
skills.definition: This skill grants improved internal temperature control, reducing performance drop in heat or cold. It trains the body to self-correct when external conditions become extreme.

skills.name: Heat Generation
skills.definition: Through precise metabolic shifts and muscular tension, this skill allows the body to generate warmth without movement. It is the fire within—activated by breath, focus, and need.

skills.name: Rapid Cool
skills.definition: When heat becomes a threat, this skill promotes swift internal cooling. It lowers risk of overheating and allows performance in conditions that would fell the untrained.

skills.name: Efficient Sweating
skills.definition: This skill reflects improved water balance and toxin release via sweat. The body cools faster, wastes less fluid, and maintains stamina in prolonged heat exposure.

skills.name: Recovery & Rest
skills.definition: Healing is not just passive—it’s an art. This skill reflects the body’s capacity to recover from fatigue, injury, and stress faster and more effectively than average. With proper rest and regulation, resilience becomes renewal.

skills.name: Accelerated Healing
skills.definition: This skill trains the body to prioritize tissue repair, immune activation, and cellular recovery. Cuts close quicker, soreness fades faster, and wounds mend with surprising speed. Healing becomes a reliable response, not a waiting game.

skills.name: Rapid Clot
skills.definition: The moment blood flows, the body acts. Rapid Clot enables near-instant sealing of minor wounds, reducing blood loss and infection risk even in harsh environments. It’s the difference between limping away and bleeding out.

skills.name: Scar Reduction
skills.definition: Healing leaves fewer marks. This skill refines tissue regrowth and collagen control, minimizing scar formation over time and improving skin and joint recovery. The body repairs with precision, not just speed.

skills.name: Tissue Regeneration
skills.definition: Beyond simple healing, this skill accelerates the rebuilding of muscles, tendons, and even deeper tissue. Recovery after damage is faster, cleaner, and more complete. It is the groundwork for total restoration.

skills.name: Sleep Efficiency
skills.definition: Sleep isn’t just about time—it’s about quality. This skill improves the body's ability to enter and benefit from deep rest, even with limited hours available. Recovery continues even when time is scarce.

skills.name: Micro Sleep
skills.definition: This skill trains the brain to extract restorative value from short bursts of rest. Even a few minutes of stillness can replenish alertness and mental clarity. It’s a tactical recharge—perfect for moments when sleep is scarce but focus is essential.

skills.name: REM Maximisation
skills.definition: The most critical sleep cycle becomes more accessible and sustained. REM Maximisation enhances dream-state recovery, benefiting memory, emotion, and mental fatigue. It compresses full-body recovery into minimal time.

skills.name: Circadian Reset
skills.definition: Whether adjusting to night shifts or jet lag, this skill allows the body to quickly realign its internal clock. Sleep comes when it's needed—day or night. Rest is no longer at the mercy of schedule or sun.

skills.name: Inflammation Control
skills.definition: Swelling is the body's alarm—but unchecked, it becomes a burden. This skill moderates internal inflammation, reducing pain, stiffness, and recovery time after stress or injury. It preserves function while accelerating healing.

skills.name: Edema Reduction
skills.definition: Water retention and puffiness are cleared with precision. This skill promotes lymphatic flow and blood return, minimizing swelling and restoring mobility. Whether after injury or exertion, the body clears excess fluid with practiced efficiency.

skills.name: Anti Oxidation
skills.definition: Cellular decay is slowed through enhanced free radical management. Anti Oxidation improves recovery from metabolic stress and extends long-term tissue health. It’s restoration at the molecular level.

skills.name: Pain Modulation
skills.definition: This skill tunes the body's internal chemistry to reduce CHAonic pain from inflammation. The edge softens, allowing movement, clarity, and rest to return. It’s a calm layered over internal chaos.

skills.name: Research & Analysis
skills.definition: This foundational skill reflects a mind built to uncover truth through study, observation, and methodical inquiry. Whether poring over books, databases, or field evidence, it represents the ability to extract meaning from information. The researcher is both detective and scholar—trained to see what others overlook.

skills.name: Library Research
skills.definition: This skill is honed in archives and forgotten shelves. The character can efficiently navigate complex collections of written knowledge, extracting what’s needed while others get lost. It’s the art of finding answers buried in centuries of ink.

skills.name: Catalog Mastery
skills.definition: Knowing the systems behind the systems, this skill allows mastery of organizational structures in libraries, vaults, or record halls. With it, even misfiled or hidden knowledge can be swiftly located.

skills.name: Cross Referencing
skills.definition: This skill connects scattered facts across sources, weaving them into a clear picture. The researcher sees patterns, contradictions, and confirmations others miss—building conclusions from fraG.O.Dents.

skills.name: Rare Text ID
skills.definition: The ability to recognize valuable or obscure texts by script, language, or binding. With this skill, a character can identify works of historical, magical, or scientific importance—even if written in forgotten tongues.

skills.name: Data Mining
skills.definition: This is the ability to extract meaning from massive pools of raw information. Whether digital or analog, the mind sorts noise from signal, looking for anomalies, gaps, or gold.

skills.name: Pattern Recognition
skills.definition: The brain becomes a net that catches rhythm, anomaly, and trend. This skill allows the character to detect recurring structures and underlying causes—even in chaotic data.

skills.name: Big Data Handling
skills.definition: Vast volumes of info become manageable. This skill reflects mental organization and mental stamina to work with massive datasets without overload, collapse, or loss of detail.

skills.name: Trend Projection
skills.definition: The future leaves fingerprints. This skill allows the character to forecast likely developments by recognizing early patterns and extrapolating long-term outcomes.

skills.name: Forensic Analysis
skills.definition: This skill focuses the intellect on evidence—material, chemical, or circumstantial. It enables deep examination of clues left behind, especially in the wake of crime, sabotage, or decay.

skills.name: Evidence Chain
skills.definition: This skill tracks the origin, journey, and context of discovered materials. It maintains logical chains of custody, meaning every finding stands as usable, traceable truth.

skills.name: Trace Material ID
skills.definition: Microscopic fibers, dust, fluids, or blood—all become voices. This skill trains the eye and mind to spot and interpret the smallest evidence with forensic clarity.

skills.name: Scene Reconstruction
skills.definition: The mind becomes the lens through which a moment is reborn. With limited clues, this skill re-creates what happened, in what order, and how—even when no witness survives.

skills.name: Engineering
skills.definition: This foundational skill reflects a mind wired for invention, structure, and precision. Whether crafting machines, analyzing load-bearing designs, or solving power flow, Engineering is the language of functionality. It turns raw materials into purpose-built creations.

skills.name: Mechanical Design
skills.definition: Focused on moving parts, levers, gears, and kinetic systems, this skill turns imagination into mechanical function. It’s how blueprints are born, refined, and made real.

skills.name: Gear Tuning
skills.definition: A machine is only as smooth as its gearing. This skill allows fine adjustments to timing, resistance, and torque, maximizing efficiency and longevity.

skills.name: Kinetic Optimisation
skills.definition: The art of motion itself—this skill refines how energy is transferred, reducing waste and boosting performance. It’s not just design—it’s fluid execution of force.

skills.name: Micro Mechanisms
skills.definition: Clockwork, miniaturized linkages, or tight-space engineering—this skill grants mastery over precise, small-scale mechanics. It’s the difference between functional and flawless.

skills.name: Electrical Systems
skills.definition: Circuits, voltage, and current become a fluent language. This skill represents the design, repair, and understanding of electronic flow in devices and power structures.

skills.name: Circuit Prototyping
skills.definition: This skill reflects the ability to create functional circuit layouts by hand or tool. Whether from scrap or silicon, it brings the invisible lifeblood of power to life.

skills.name: Signal Diagnostics
skills.definition: With this skill, no short or surge goes unnoticed. It trains the mind to read and trace signal integrity across complex boards and repair what's unseen.

skills.name: Power Regulation
skills.definition: Power without control is failure waiting to happen. This skill allows the engineer to balance energy demands, preventing overload and sustaining precision output.

skills.name: Structural Fabrication
skills.definition: From bridges to bunkers, this skill covers load-bearing designs, material use, and construction principles. It builds strong from blueprint to beam.

skills.name: Load Calculation
skills.definition: Before the first brick is placed, this skill determines how much force a structure can bear. It calculates tolerances, balances stress, and prevents collapse before it starts.

skills.name: Stress Analysis
skills.definition: This skill detects points of weakness before they break. It reflects an instinct for where pressure collects and how to reinforce or redistribute it in real time.

skills.name: Modular Construction
skills.definition: Built to move, change, or expand—this skill focuses on adaptive design. Structures become systems of interlocking parts, allowing repair, upgrade, and reconfiguration in the field.

skills.name: Systems Interface
skills.definition: This skill reflects a mind capable of interfacing with mechanical, digital, or artificial systems. Whether navigating user layers or machine logic, the user doesn’t just operate systems—they understand their language. It's intuition for structure, code, and flow.

skills.name: Logic Patterning
skills.definition: Behind every function is a pattern, and this skill trains the brain to see and shape those sequences. It enables efficient scripting, clean logic loops, and error prevention through structural clarity.

skills.name: Pattern Streamlining
skills.definition: The clutter of complex logic is stripped away. This skill refines convoluted systems into lean, elegant structures—maximizing speed, function, and legibility.

skills.name: Sequence Refinement
skills.definition: This skill allows for targeted improvements within logic chains, optimizing process steps for clarity and reliability. Even if one link fails, the system can adapt and reroute.

skills.name: System Fluency
skills.definition: The system becomes second nature. This skill grants instinctual navigation and command over digital or mechanical frameworks, allowing on-the-fly configuration without hesitation.

skills.name: Intrusion Defense
skills.definition: This is the mind of a digital sentinel. Intrusion Defense enables the identification of weaknesses, unauthorized access, or corruptive inputs within any structured system.

skills.name: Vulnerability Mapping
skills.definition: This skill creates a mental overlay of system weaknesses, visually or intuitively highlighting exploit paths. It’s both offensive planning and defensive foresight.

skills.name: Exploit Engineering
skills.definition: When a system breaks, this skill decides how and where. It enables intentional crafting or prevention of code exploits, backdoors, and system overrides.

skills.name: Layered Safeguards
skills.definition: No system is secure without redundancy. This skill develops multiple layers of defense—detection, misdirection, and failsafe protocols—to prevent both brute force and stealth intrusions.

skills.name: Synthetic Cognition
skills.definition: This skill reflects the merging of thought and artificial processes. Whether AI, drone logic, or mental projection into machines, the mind shapes the synthetic without delay.

skills.name: Cognitive Shaping
skills.definition: The user can adjust how artificial minds or scripts think, react, or learn. It’s not just programming—it’s behavioral engineering for machines.

skills.name: Command Tuning
skills.definition: When systems respond to commands, this skill ensures they respond correctly and efficiently. It refines outputs, reduces latency, and adapts intent to machine-specific dialects.

skills.name: Independent Constructs
skills.definition: This skill enables the creation or mentoring of synthetic systems capable of limited self-direction. Constructs gain adaptive protocols and semi-autonomous reasoning through refined design.

skills.name: Medicine
skills.definition: Medicine is the disciplined study of the body, its ailments, and the means to heal or intervene. This foundational skill reflects clinical thinking, emergency responsiveness, and anatomical understanding. Whether in sterile hospitals or muddy fields, knowledge becomes life-saving action.

skills.name: First Aid
skills.definition: The immediate response to injury or illness before advanced care arrives. First Aid emphasizes stabilization, control, and efficiency under pressure—turning panic into precision.

skills.name: Haemorrhage Control
skills.definition: When blood flows fast, this skill slows it. It reflects rapid tourniquet use, pressure point knowledge, and clotting techniques that prevent fatal blood loss in the critical window.

skills.name: Fracture Stabilisation
skills.definition: Bones may break, but movement must stop. This skill enables splinting, alignment, and immobilization to reduce further injury until full treatment is possible.

skills.name: Triage Prioritisation
skills.definition: When many are wounded, choices must be made. This skill identifies who can be saved, who needs immediate help, and who must wait—ensuring resources are spent wisely.

skills.name: Clinical Diagnostics
skills.definition: This skill reflects the ability to interpret signs, symptoms, and test results to reach an accurate diagnosis. It’s part science, part deduction, and part intuition honed by study.

skills.name: Symptom Differential
skills.definition: Every cough isn't the same. This skill isolates similar symptoms to determine root causes, narrowing the possibilities with clarity and precision.

skills.name: Lab Test Interpretation
skills.definition: From blood work to imaging results, this skill extracts meaning from data. It turns numbers and visuals into actionable medical insight.

skills.name: Pharmacology Dosing
skills.definition: Medicines are only as safe as their dosages. This skill reflects precise understanding of administration, interaction, and body weight response—avoiding both under-treatment and overdose.

skills.name: Surgical Procedure
skills.definition: A steady hand and a sharp mind—this skill enables invasive intervention with practiced care. It covers pre-op setup, field readiness, and core technique understanding.

skills.name: Incision Precision
skills.definition: Every cut must count. This skill represents surgical accuracy—minimizing damage, blood loss, and recovery time through exact blade placement.

skills.name: Suture Technique
skills.definition: Flesh needs stitching, and this skill provides the finesse to do it cleanly. From battlefield closures to operating table threads, it ensures wounds are sealed and safe.

skills.name: Field Sterilisation
skills.definition: Infection is as deadly as injury. This skill allows improvisation of sterile environments and tools in non-clinical settings—preserving safety when standards can’t be guaranteed.

skills.name: Chemistry
skills.definition: Chemistry is the study of substances, their transformations, and the forces behind those changes. This skill allows the user to manipulate matter on a molecular level, crafting, combining, and breaking down with intent. Whether in lab or field, knowledge becomes reaction.

skills.name: Synthesis
skills.definition: This skill represents the creation of compounds from simpler materials. Whether concocting a medicine, alloy, or adhesive, it allows for intentional molecular assembly.

skills.name: Organic Synthesis
skills.definition: Focused on carbon-based chemistry, this skill enables the crafting of biochemicals, plant extracts, and medicinal reagents. The user navigates complex reactions with biological precision.

skills.name: Polymeric Casting
skills.definition: Plastics, resins, and elastomers take shape under trained hands. This skill covers the controlled formation and molding of long-chain molecules for use in tools, armor, or insulation.

skills.name: Compound Purity
skills.definition: Impurities ruin function. This skill refines the user’s ability to isolate and perfect substances—ensuring stable reactions and maximized potency in every result.

skills.name: Analysis
skills.definition: The counterpart to synthesis, this skill focuses on identifying substances through observation and testing. It breaks the unknown into known components.

skills.name: Spectrography
skills.definition: Using light to decode matter, this skill allows precise identification of elements and compounds via spectral signature. Even trace amounts reveal themselves to a trained eye.

skills.name: Titration Mastery
skills.definition: A careful drop at a time, this skill balances acid, base, and beyond to reveal concentrations and reaction points. It reflects patience, control, and accuracy under chemical tension.

skills.name: CHAomatography
skills.definition: This skill enables the separation and study of mixtures through flowing media. Whether ink, blood, or poison, components reveal themselves in layered color and structure.

skills.name: Energetics
skills.definition: Focused on energy release and control, this skill handles the chemistry of combustion, explosion, and propulsion. It is fire wielded with intellect.

skills.name: Propellant Formulation
skills.definition: This skill designs fuel sources—solid, liquid, or hybrid—with exacting balance. Burn rate, thrust, and containment are engineered into reliable performance.

skills.name: Controlled Detonation
skills.definition: Not all explosions are chaotic. This skill ensures timing, force, and direction are all intentional, reducing risk while maximizing result. Precision meets power.

skills.name: Stability Testing
skills.definition: Heat, impact, pressure—this skill determines what a compound can survive. It prevents accidents and ensures that volatile materials are handled with the respect they demand.

skills.name: Cryptography & Linguistics
skills.definition: This skill reflects mastery over language, symbol, and secrecy. It encompasses the decoding of ancient tongues, creation of private scripts, and manipulation of meaning across cultures. Where others see noise, this mind sees structure.

skills.name: Language Deciphering
skills.definition: From runes to dialects, this skill allows understanding of unfamiliar languages through pattern, root, and sound. It’s decoding without a dictionary—reconstructing meaning from exposure.

skills.name: Dead Tongue Reading
skills.definition: Forgotten languages leave clues. This skill enables reading and partial reconstruction of extinct or lost tongues from fraG.O.Dents, syntax echoes, and linguistic lineage.

skills.name: Accent Acquisition
skills.definition: Mimicry meets fluency—this skill allows the speaker to rapidly adapt pronunciation, cadence, and inflection. It smooths communication and masks origin.

skills.name: Rapid Translation
skills.definition: With only brief exposure, the user can grasp context and meaning from foreign speech or script. This skill accelerates comprehension, even across unfamiliar or hybrid languages.

skills.name: Code Making
skills.definition: This skill creates secure systems of hidden meaning. From encryption protocols to secret alphabets, it ensures only the intended reader can unlock the message.

skills.name: Cipher Construction
skills.definition: The bones of encryption. This skill enables the creation of substitution, transposition, or hybrid ciphers with layers of defense. Each system built is its own puzzle.

skills.name: One Time Pad
skills.definition: This advanced cryptographic method uses random keys that can never be reused or cracked. With this skill, near-perfect secrecy is achieved—if the key is never compromised.

skills.name: Steganographic Embed
skills.definition: Hiding messages in plain sight—within images, texts, or audio—this skill allows for concealment so subtle it escapes detection entirely. A word within a pattern, a whisper in the static.

skills.name: Symbol Interpretation
skills.definition: From graffiti to cave etchings, this skill extracts meaning from nonverbal signs. It deciphers symbols, pictographs, and glyphs by form, placement, and cultural logic.

skills.name: Semiotic Analysis
skills.definition: This skill breaks down how signs and symbols generate meaning. It allows deep understanding of context, usage, and embedded ideology—critical in decoding constructed messages.

skills.name: Cultural Context
skills.definition: Symbols only matter if you know the world they came from. This skill ties markings and words to the belief systems, fears, and values of the people who made them.

skills.name: Pattern Keying
skills.definition: Where repeated shapes, rhythms, or structures exist, this skill identifies them and reveals hidden layers of meaning. It’s the key that unlocks the pattern inside the symbol.

skills.name: Tactical Planning
skills.definition: Tactical Planning is the mental framework for anticipating, organizing, and executing action in dynamic environments. It balances short-term precision with long-term vision, ensuring preparedness under shifting threats. Every move is made with layers of purpose.

skills.name: Strategy Modelling
skills.definition: This skill enables simulation of possible outcomes based on known assets, terrain, and behavior. Strategy Modelling visualizes moves before they happen—building winning plans before the first step.

skills.name: Battlefield Sim
skills.definition: Whether virtual or mental, this skill constructs battlefield scenarios with live variables. It allows pre-action testing of tactics, revealing blind spots and opportunities alike.

skills.name: Risk Assessment
skills.definition: No plan survives without evaluation. This skill identifies where failure is most likely, weighing cost, timing, and benefit to strengthen decision-making under pressure.

skills.name: Object Prioritisation
skills.definition: In chaos, focus becomes survival. This skill enables rapid triage of goals, threats, and objectives—ensuring what matters most is handled first.

skills.name: Resource Allocation
skills.definition: This skill turns limited assets into strategic advantage. Whether assigning people, gear, or time, it ensures nothing is wasted and every tool has a purpose.

skills.name: Supply Chain Map
skills.definition: Knowing where things are is as vital as having them. This skill visualizes the full supply path from source to use, exposing breaks, delays, or excess.

skills.name: Efficiency Analysis
skills.definition: This skill tracks performance and waste across operations. By identifying lag, overuse, or redundancy, it sharpens systems to run lean and strong.

skills.name: Redundant Routing
skills.definition: Backup paths, alternate drop points, or fallback logistics—this skill designs secondary flows to prevent mission failure if the primary plan breaks.

skills.name: Contingency Crafting
skills.definition: The unexpected is expected. This skill enables the creation of backup plans layered into the main strategy, ready to activate if things go wrong.

skills.name: Fail Safe Design
skills.definition: This skill embeds protections into systems, operations, or tech to prevent catastrophic failure. Even in collapse, it ensures something still works.

skills.name: Exit Route Recognition
skills.definition: Knowing when and how to leave is as strategic as knowing when to strike. This skill maps retreat paths, extraction zones, or fallback positions—always with time to act.

skills.name: Adaptive Response
skills.definition: This skill trains flexible thinking in live conditions. When plans fail, it enables rapid recalibration without hesitation—turning failure into improvisation.

skills.name: Innovation
skills.definition: Innovation is the art of applied creativity—taking the imagined and making it real. It covers conceptual breakthroughs, adaptive solutions, and systems that didn't exist until now. The innovator solves what others accept.

skills.name: Prototype Ideation
skills.definition: This skill captures the early stages of invention—sketching, modeling, and mentally testing new designs. It's not about polish, but about having the boldness to begin.

skills.name: Rapid Prototype
skills.definition: When time is tight, this skill builds functional models fast. Whether from scrap or kit, it gets the concept in-hand and working with minimal delay.

skills.name: Proof of Concept
skills.definition: An idea means little until it functions. This skill validates whether a design can do what it claims, isolating the core test that proves viability.

skills.name: Design Iteration
skills.definition: The first version is never final. This skill enables fast, efficient tweaks to an idea—reworking flaws, improving strengths, and driving version after version forward.

skills.name: Improvisation
skills.definition: With limited tools or materials, the innovator adapts. This skill represents quick thinking, unconventional use of parts, and making things work under pressure.

skills.name: Jury Rig
skills.definition: When systems fail, this skill gets them running again—maybe ugly, but functional. Jury Rig reflects fast fixes, stopgap solutions, and field-ready patchwork.

skills.name: Field Repair
skills.definition: Beyond the workshop, this skill keeps gear alive under rough conditions. From broken drones to cracked helmets, the innovator restores function when failure isn’t an option.

skills.name: Material Substitution
skills.definition: When the ideal part isn’t available, the innovator finds an alternative. This skill allows for safe, effective swaps using local, salvaged, or even organic components.

skills.name: Optimisation
skills.definition: Every system has room to improve. This skill sharpens designs, workflows, or outputs for greater effectiveness—often through small, elegant changes.

skills.name: Process Streamline
skills.definition: This skill identifies bottlenecks and oversteps in any process. It trims excess and accelerates outcomes, turning a good system into a great one.

skills.name: Cost Reduction
skills.definition: Efficiency is more than speed—it’s also expense. This skill finds ways to produce the same result with fewer resources, lower energy, or simpler inputs.

skills.name: Performance Boost
skills.definition: Every system can go further. This skill extracts more output, power, or reliability from existing designs—without needing to rebuild from scratch.

skills.name: Channeling
skills.definition: Channeling grants your character the ability to act as a conduit for supernatural energy. Whether divine, arcane, or elemental, this skill determines how much mana or power can be safely drawn into the body. The more practiced the channel, the more intense and sustained the force they can wield. Required to effectively channel magic from any sphere.

skills.name: Meditation
skills.definition: Meditation cultivates internal stillness and spiritual clarity. Through this practice, your character recovers mana, centers their will, and fortifies their connection to unseen energies. Meditation is required to restore mana after magical exertion.

skills.name: Spellcraft
skills.definition: Spellcraft is the intellectual mastery of magical form and function. With it, characters access magical spheres and manipulate spell structures with intent and precision. This skill grants access to magical spheres for casting spells.

skills.name: Talismanism
skills.definition: Talismanism is the art of embedding magic into objects—stones, metals, parchment, or bone. It grants access to the same spheres as spellcraft, but through crafted focus rather than cast command. This skill grants access to magical spheres for talisman-based use.

skills.name: Perception
skills.definition: Perception reflects your ability to notice, interpret, and respond to subtle cues in your surroundings. It governs all sensory input—sight, sound, motion, and presence—and helps detect what others overlook. Whether watching from afar or catching a whisper nearby, this skill tunes the mind to the world.

skills.name: Visual Acuity
skills.definition: This skill hones the eyes to spot details, shifts, and movement others miss. Visual Acuity enables clearer recognition at distance, in motion, or under stress. It's about seeing with intent, not just eyesight.

skills.name: Long Sight
skills.definition: Long Sight allows recognition of distant shapes, figures, or terrain features with remarkable clarity. It extends the visual horizon beyond normal range.

skills.name: Low Light Vision
skills.definition: This skill enhances the ability to see in dim or near-dark environments without relying on artificial light. It draws on natural adaptation and trained sensory recall.

skills.name: Motion Detection
skills.definition: With Motion Detection, even the slightest shift draws attention. This skill allows your eyes to prioritize movement instinctively, useful for tracking, spotting ambush, or trailing prey.

skills.name: Auditory Acuity
skills.definition: Auditory Acuity sharpens hearing to catch whispers, shifts in tone, and subtle environmental cues. It trains the ear to isolate sound and react to it with precision.

skills.name: Acute Hearing
skills.definition: This skill pushes hearing to its limit—identifying small sounds, distant footsteps, or quiet speech in noisy environments.

skills.name: Frequency Filter
skills.definition: Frequency Filter allows the brain to ignore background noise and tune into specific audio ranges. It’s like having an internal noise gate.

skills.name: Echo Read
skills.definition: Echo Read interprets sound reflections to detect spatial changes or unseen movement. It enables primitive echolocation-style awareness.

skills.name: Situational Awareness
skills.definition: Situational Awareness connects sensory information into a unified field of understanding. It represents alertness to threat, position, and opportunity from all directions.

skills.name: Peripheral Alert
skills.definition: Peripheral Alert trains the mind to process input from the edges of vision. It catches side movement, shifting shapes, and sudden threats without full focus.

skills.name: Quick Scan
skills.definition: This skill allows a fast but meaningful sweep of an environment, noting exits, individuals, or danger in mere moments.

skills.name: Blind Spot Detection
skills.definition: The user develops an instinctive sense for what isn’t being watched. This skill identifies zones of vulnerability in combat, patrol, or observation.

skills.name: Insight
skills.definition: Insight allows your character to read beneath the surface of words, actions, and silence. It reflects an intuitive grasp of motive, truth, and future consequence. From body language to tone shifts, it turns subtle clues into deep understanding.

skills.name: Motive Reading
skills.definition: This skill focuses on deciphering why someone is acting the way they are. It detects concealed goals, internal conflict, or emotional pressure that shapes behavior.

skills.name: Emotion Decoding
skills.definition: This skill isolates emotional states based on micro-expressions, tone, or gesture. It reads what’s felt even when nothing is said.

skills.name: Agenda Exposing
skills.definition: With this skill, characters can unravel long-term motives or secret goals. It senses not just what someone wants now—but where they’re heading.

skills.name: Pattern Spotting
skills.definition: This skill recognizes behavioral repetition and hidden cycles in speech, movement, or decision-making. It’s the tool for reading people like puzzles.

skills.name: Lie Detection
skills.definition: This skill trains perception to catch moments of dishonesty or distortion. Whether through tension, over-explaining, or misplaced details, deception leaves clues.

skills.name: Pulse Check
skills.definition: Pulse Check reads stress responses—voice shifts, heart rate cues, or physical tells—under questioning. It's not proof, but it's strong suspicion.

skills.name: Verbal Tell
skills.definition: This skill analyzes word choice, filler, and sentence structure to reveal discomfort or deception. The truth struggles to hide in speech.

skills.name: Body Language Reading
skills.definition: Posture, fidgeting, gaze—this skill interprets physical cues that betray truth or falsehood. The body speaks what the mouth avoids.

skills.name: Intuitive Forecast
skills.definition: This skill sharpens instinct toward what’s coming next. It takes small signals—social, environmental, emotional—and makes fast internal predictions.

skills.name: Gut Alert
skills.definition: Gut Alert triggers when something is wrong—timing, words, or atmosphere. It’s an emotional warning, backed by unseen patterns.

skills.name: Best Guess Projection
skills.definition: This skill forms logical predictions based on limited information. It’s the art of filling gaps in real time with tactical intuition.

skills.name: Probability Reading
skills.definition: The user becomes skilled in weighing outcomes, assigning risk, and sensing which path is most likely. It is not calculation—it is practiced foresight.

skills.name: Survival
skills.definition: Survival reflects the ability to live, travel, and adapt in wilderness environments without dependence on civilization. It includes the instincts and training needed to locate resources, read nature, construct shelter, and endure unpredictable conditions. A skilled survivor turns harsh terrain into home, and danger into routine.

skills.name: Tracking
skills.definition: This skill allows a character to identify and follow trails left by people, animals, or creatures. It involves reading broken twigs, altered paths, disturbed ground, and even air currents. Tracking is the art of seeing stories written in the dirt.

skills.name: Footprint ID
skills.definition: Footprint ID allows the user to determine size, species, gait, and even emotional state from a single track. Through precise interpretation, it reveals everything from weight and gear to urgency or injury.

skills.name: Scent Trail
skills.definition: This skill enables following a trail through lingering scent markers—natural or artificial. Whether tracking blood, sweat, pheromones, or smoke, the user can isolate and pursue a specific target over time.

skills.name: Long Trail Pursuit
skills.definition: Long Trail Pursuit grants endurance and clarity when following a trail over vast terrain or across days. It includes interpreting environmental shifts, rerouted paths, and older or disrupted trails.

skills.name: Wilderness Foraging
skills.definition: Foraging involves identifying and collecting edible, medicinal, or useful plants and resources in the wild. This skill separates nourishment from poison, resource from danger. A true forager feeds others without ever drawing steel.

skills.name: Edible Plant ID
skills.definition: This skill provides encyclopedic familiarity with wild flora. It enables recognition of safe, nourishing plants even in unknown regions, including seasonal shifts and regional variants.

skills.name: Water Locate
skills.definition: Water Locate grants the ability to read terrain, vegetation, animal behavior, and geological signs to uncover hidden or distant sources of fresh water. In dry places, this is life magic without the spell.

skills.name: Trap Setting
skills.definition: Trap Setting involves crafting and placing snares, deadfalls, trip lines, or other mechanisms to catch animals or deter intruders. It combines environmental blending with mechanical precision.

skills.name: Sheltercraft
skills.definition: Sheltercraft teaches how to build protective structures using natural or salvaged materials. It includes insulation, wind resistance, concealment, and time-efficient construction. Shelter is survival’s first breath.

skills.name: Quick Shelter
skills.definition: Quick Shelter emphasizes speed and necessity. It enables rapid deployment of functional shelters using minimal tools—ideal during storms, combat retreats, or exhaustion.

skills.name: Camouflage Camp
skills.definition: Camouflage Camp blends the campsite into the environment, hiding signs of fire, scent, waste, or movement. This skill is favored by those who know eyes are always watching.

skills.name: Hazard Proofing
skills.definition: Hazard Proofing reinforces shelter against fire, predators, flood, rockfall, or collapse. It includes smart site selection, pressure release techniques, and layered defense.

skills.name: Investigation
skills.definition: Investigation is the pursuit of truth through focused observation, questioning, and deduction. It combines logic, intuition, and method to uncover hidden facts and reconstruct events. Whether probing a crime scene or unraveling a lie, this skill brings darkness into the light.

skills.name: Evidence Gathering
skills.definition: This skill covers the physical collection and preservation of clues from any location. From footprints to fibers, it ensures no detail is lost—and nothing is contaminated.

skills.name: Trace Collection
skills.definition: Trace Collection focuses on retrieving microscopic or residual materials: hairs, dust, fibers, ash, and more. It detects what others would overlook.

skills.name: Tool Mark ID
skills.definition: This skill allows the identification of marks left by tools, weapons, or forced entry. It reconstructs what object was used, how it was used, and by whom.

skills.name: Scene Mapping
skills.definition: Scene Mapping organizes all found evidence spatially—recreating the exact layout of events as they unfolded. It turns chaos into a readable timeline.

skills.name: Interview Technique
skills.definition: Interview Technique covers the process of questioning witnesses or suspects. It involves tone control, observation, and eliciting useful information without overt pressure.

skills.name: Rapport Building
skills.definition: Rapport Building creates emotional connection and trust quickly, even under tense or hostile circumstances. It opens people up—without them realizing.

skills.name: Micro Expression Reading
skills.definition: This skill analyzes momentary facial cues that betray emotion: surprise, anger, fear, deceit. It’s the investigator’s window into unspoken truth.

skills.name: Statement Analysis
skills.definition: Statement Analysis breaks down language patterns to expose inconsistency, bias, or evasion. The choice of words often reveals more than their meaning.

skills.name: Analytical Profiling
skills.definition: This skill enables the construction of behavioral profiles based on habits, choices, and triggers. It creates a psychological footprint of the unknown.

skills.name: Behavior Pattern
skills.definition: This skill identifies recurring behaviors across time, space, or victims. It shows method, repetition, and evolution—often invisible at first glance.

skills.name: MO Correlation
skills.definition: MO Correlation links actions or crimes by method, signature, or motive. It connects scattered clues across distance or time.

skills.name: Predictive Modeling
skills.definition: Using current evidence and behavior analysis, this skill predicts likely next moves or outcomes. It’s less about certainty, and more about calculated probability.

skills.name: Animal Handling
skills.definition: Animal Handling reflects a deep understanding of animal behavior, needs, and communication. It includes building trust, controlling movement, calming fear, and bonding across species. Whether in stable, field, or wild, this skill turns instinct into cooperation.

skills.name: Training
skills.definition: Training teaches creatures repeatable tasks through reinforcement, command, and patience. It transforms potential into dependable action.

skills.name: Obedience Drill
skills.definition: This skill instills absolute compliance to verbal or gestural commands. It creates dependable, disciplined companions ready to act without hesitation.

skills.name: Trick Conditioning
skills.definition: Trick Conditioning enables the teaching of complex or performative behaviors for show, distraction, or utility. From bowing to bell-ringing, intelligence is awakened.

skills.name: Pack Coordination
skills.definition: This skill synCHAonizes multiple animals for cooperative behavior—movement, defense, or formation. The handler becomes a silent conductor of the pack.

skills.name: Riding
skills.definition: Riding covers the ability to mount, direct, and stay balanced on domesticated or semi-domesticated animals. It includes awareness of gait, behavior, and terrain response.

skills.name: Bareback Mastery
skills.definition: This skill allows full control and balance without tack or saddle. It emphasizes harmony with the animal’s rhythm and movement.

skills.name: Mounted Combat
skills.definition: Mounted Combat grants the ability to fight from atop a creature—timing strikes, pivoting, and directing while moving. It turns rider and mount into one force.

skills.name: Rapid Dismount
skills.definition: This skill allows for safe, controlled, and swift dismount from a moving or rearing mount. It minimizes risk and enables fast transition to ground combat or escape.

skills.name: Beast Empathy
skills.definition: Beast Empathy builds intuitive rapport with creatures through scent, posture, tone, and presence. It opens communication without dominance.

skills.name: Calm Beast
skills.definition: Calm Beast diffuses fear, aggression, or panic in animals through subtle cues and confident energy. The wild learns to trust.

skills.name: Scent Blend
skills.definition: This skill masks or modifies the user’s natural scent to reduce threat, trigger familiarity, or blend with a herd.

skills.name: Pack Communing
skills.definition: Pack Communing allows mutual nonverbal understanding within bonded creatures. It enables mood sensing, silent direction, and shared instinct in the field.

skills.name: Mental Fortitude
skills.definition: Mental Fortitude is the inner armor of the mind—the ability to withstand fear, resist manipulation, and maintain clarity under duress. It reflects emotional control, psychological resilience, and mental discipline. A strong mind does not break—it bends and returns sharper.

skills.name: Resolve
skills.definition: Resolve builds resistance against internal collapse. It shields against panic, dread, sorrow, or confusion when the world becomes overwhelming.

skills.name: Fear Resistance
skills.definition: Fear Resistance enables action in the presence of terror. It suppresses the instinct to freeze or flee and allows thought to continue when the heart races.

skills.name: Despair Shield
skills.definition: This skill strengthens willpower against despair, hopelessness, or emotional sabotage. It helps the user hold their center when all seems lost.

skills.name: Pain Gate
skills.definition: Pain Gate filters the body’s pain signals before they reach the conscious mind. It doesn’t erase pain—but it places it behind a locked door.

skills.name: Concentration
skills.definition: Concentration trains the mind to anchor itself to a single thought or task, excluding all else. It turns awareness into a laser instead of a floodlight.

skills.name: Selective Focus
skills.definition: Selective Focus allows the user to tune in to one stream of input while ignoring all others—perfect for combat, study, or meditation.

skills.name: Distraction Ignoring
skills.definition: This skill builds immunity to ambient noise, visual clutter, and emotional interruption. It protects attention in chaotic or hostile environments.

skills.name: Meditation Lock
skills.definition: Meditation Lock enables a deep mental state that cannot be broken easily. Even under stress, the user can remain in inner stillness until they choose to return.

skills.name: Lucid Dreaming
skills.definition: Lucid Dreaming allows awareness and agency within sleep. It grants memory of dreams, control over dreamspace, and defense against psychic intrusion.

skills.name: Dream Recall
skills.definition: This skill preserves vivid, complete memories of dream experiences—turning fleeting images into usable knowledge or clues.

skills.name: Dream Control
skills.definition: Dream Control allows conscious influence over dreams: shaping events, summoning symbols, or directing outcomes while asleep.

skills.name: Nightmare Ban
skills.definition: This skill enables the user to confront or dispel nightmares—whether psychological, magical, or foreign in origin. Fear has no dominion where the dreamer commands.

skills.name: Navigation
skills.definition: Navigation is the ability to find your way across wilderness, cities, or unknown terrain using landmarks, tools, or instinct. It includes orienteering, mental mapping, and orientation in both natural and constructed spaces. A skilled navigator is rarely lost—and never for long.

skills.name: Wayfinding
skills.definition: Wayfinding is the practical art of moving across distance with direction and purpose. It relies on reading terrain, sun, stars, water flow, and other natural signs to maintain accurate travel.

skills.name: Dead Reckoning
skills.definition: This skill allows navigation based on last known location, movement speed, and elapsed time—without the need for visible landmarks. It’s used in fog, night, or dense environments.

skills.name: Terrestrial Compass
skills.definition: Terrestrial Compass grants intuitive orientation, allowing the user to instinctively feel cardinal directions—even when disoriented, underground, or magically disrupted.

skills.name: Trailblazer
skills.definition: Trailblazer grants the ability to forge new paths through wild terrain while minimizing hazard and leaving clear markers. It’s not just finding the way—it’s creating one.

skills.name: Cartography
skills.definition: Cartography is the structured recording and interpretation of terrain through maps, sketches, or glyphs. It allows travelers to leave information behind or share it with others.

skills.name: Rapid Sketch Map
skills.definition: This skill allows quick creation of functional field maps—usable even when rushed, fatigued, or under pressure. Clarity over beauty.

skills.name: Surveying Grid
skills.definition: Surveying Grid introduces mathematical precision—aligning map details to consistent scales and coordinates. Essential for construction, defense, or advanced planning.

skills.name: Terrain Mapping
skills.definition: Terrain Mapping creates detailed elevation and obstacle diagrams, useful for travel, siege, or escape planning. It sees not just the place—but how the place works.

skills.name: Urban Orientation
skills.definition: Urban Orientation trains the mind to track spatial relationships in dense, shifting environments. It maps buildings, alleys, sewer grates, and choke points in memory.

skills.name: Subsurface Routing
skills.definition: This skill grants an instinctive understanding of tunnels, catacombs, basements, and underground transit paths. Perfect for infiltration, escape, or smuggling.

skills.name: Shortcut Analysis
skills.definition: Shortcut Analysis spots fast or hidden routes others overlook—gaps in fences, alley angles, or rooftop paths. Every space becomes a tool.

skills.name: Evacuation Pathing
skills.definition: This skill enables rapid creation of exit plans in real time—accounting for crowds, blockages, or environmental collapse. It’s used by those who expect things to go wrong.

skills.name: Herbal Lore
skills.definition: Herbal Lore is the ancient study of plants and their hidden powers—curative, toxic, or alchemical. It requires both intuition and experience to safely work with herbs across regions, climates, and seasons. A master of this skill sees medicine where others see weeds, and poison where others see beauty. The land itself becomes both a toolkit and a weapon.

skills.name: Medicinal Herbs
skills.definition: Medicinal Herbs focuses on plants that restore, soothe, or cleanse. From poultices to teas, it teaches how to extract life-giving properties from nature’s bounty. This skill forms the core of natural healing without magic.

skills.name: Antiseptic Application
skills.definition: This skill allows for the creation of topical mixtures that prevent infection, reduce swelling, and sterilize wounds. It emphasizes clean preparation, sterile wrapping, and field-ready treatments. The difference between rot and recovery often lies here.

skills.name: Analgesic Mix
skills.definition: Analgesic Mix covers the creation of pain-relieving formulas through oils, tinctures, and compresses. It teaches precise dosage, delayed release, and targeted effect—relief without clouding judG.O.Dent.

skills.name: Healing Poultice
skills.definition: A Healing Poultice draws infection, closes minor wounds, and accelerates natural recovery. This skill blends texture, heat, and herb layering to maximize effectiveness during rest. It is the healer’s silent ally.

skills.name: Toxic Botany
skills.definition: Toxic Botany is the art of identifying and harnessing dangerous plants for offensive or defensive use. It explores paralysis, nausea, confusion, and even death—through seeds, saps, and spores. This knowledge walks the edge of taboo.

skills.name: Neurotoxin Extraction
skills.definition: This skill isolates precise chemical agents that disrupt nerve signals—leading to paralysis, spasms, or failure of control. Extraction requires ritual, timing, and deadly accuracy.

skills.name: Irritant Dust
skills.definition: Irritant Dust creates airborne powders that blind, choke, sting, or disorient. It is used for nonlethal control, escape tactics, or distraction. The skill includes drying, grinding, and blending agents to trigger biological reflexes.

skills.name: Paralytic Concoction
skills.definition: This skill enables creation of mixtures that lock joints, slow movement, or collapse muscle function temporarily. Used correctly, it leaves a target helpless but alive.

skills.name: Alchemical Reagents
skills.definition: Alchemical Reagents are herbs refined for use in potions, enchantments, or chemical reactions. This skill teaches solvent pairing, heat reaction, and component isolation for high purity. It blends science with instinct.

skills.name: Solvent Infusion
skills.definition: Solvent Infusion suspends an herb’s active essence into oil, alcohol, or acid, allowing long-term storage or concentrated release. It’s the cornerstone of potioncraft and ritual use.

skills.name: Catalyst Powder
skills.definition: Catalyst Powders spark reactions—magical or chemical—when added to brews, flasks, or spells. This skill demands precision milling, drying, and activation timing.

skills.name: Essence Distillation
skills.definition: This skill extracts a plant’s purest effect into liquid or crystalline form. It is delicate, powerful, and volatile—used for rituals, elite potions, or rare salves. Only the most disciplined dare push this far.

skills.name: Dreamcraft
skills.definition: Dreamcraft is the conscious exploration and manipulation of dreams—whether your own or others’. It bridges the waking and unconscious worlds, revealing hidden meanings, memories, and futures. Those skilled in Dreamcraft walk through their mind as if it were terrain, and shape dreamspace as others shape clay.

skills.name: Dream Interpretation
skills.definition: Dream Interpretation focuses on reading and decoding symbolic language within dreams. It discerns what images mean emotionally, spiritually, or prophetically. This is the key to unlocking messages woven in metaphor.

skills.name: Symbol Decoding
skills.definition: This skill grants deep fluency in interpreting dream-symbols based on culture, personal resonance, or archetypal structure. A sword isn’t always a weapon—sometimes it’s guilt, power, or protection.

skills.name: Emotional Resonance
skills.definition: This skill detects the emotional truth behind dream elements, even when disguised by imagery. It reveals what the dreamer feels but cannot name.

skills.name: Memory Unseal
skills.definition: Memory Unseal allows repressed or forgotten experiences to rise into dreamspace. Through careful reflection and guidance, the sleeper recalls what was lost—or hidden.

skills.name: Lucid Dream Navigation
skills.definition: Lucid Dream Navigation grants control within the dream. It allows movement between dream layers, shaping of surroundings, and preservation of awareness while sleeping.

skills.name: Dreamwalk Control
skills.definition: This skill allows conscious direction of action, location, and interaction within dreams. Whether flying, hiding, or confronting, the dream becomes a chosen path.

skills.name: Dream Door Forge
skills.definition: Dream Door Forge creates gateways within the dream—portals to memories, other dreamers, or archetypal realms. Used by mystics, walkers, and dreambound.

skills.name: Nightmare Override
skills.definition: This skill grants the power to confront, reshape, or banish nightmares at will. It prevents fear from seizing control of dreamspace—and reclaims sovereignty.

skills.name: Prophetic Dreaming
skills.definition: Prophetic Dreaming tunes the mind to dreams that reveal fraG.O.Dents of the future. It may arrive as symbol, scene, or feeling—but the message is real. This is not control; it is reception.

skills.name: Premonition Trigger
skills.definition: This skill increases the frequency and clarity of foresight dreams, often through ritual, herbs, or intention. It awakens the sleeping mind to possibility.

skills.name: Parallel Vision
skills.definition: Parallel Vision allows the dreamer to glimpse simultaneous outcomes, timelines, or selves. It grants insight into the consequences of choice before waking.

skills.name: Destiny Thread
skills.definition: Destiny Thread reveals the single, strongest line through fate within a dream. It grants the dreamer a glimpse of the pattern pulling all else forward—if they can accept its truth.

skills.name: Faith
skills.definition: Faith is unwavering conviction rooted in belief, devotion, or sacred law. It represents spiritual certainty and inner alignment with divine principles. This skill is required for access to magical spheres via the path of Faith.

skills.name: Prayer
skills.definition: Prayer is the disciplined act of speaking, reaching, or listening to divine presence. Through it, a character may call on spheres granted by their deity, receiving insight, strength, or magical effect. This is your sphere access skill for the Faith path.

skills.name: Devotion
skills.definition: Devotion is sustained spiritual focus—ritual, mantra, or obedience—that strengthens the soul’s connection to its source. It multiplies the power of one's Faith during magical or divine restoration. Used with Faith to determine mana recovery.

skills.name: Psionic Focus
skills.definition: Psionic Focus trains the mind to exclude all noise and lock onto a singular goal. It is mental stillness forged into action. Required for access to psionic disciplines.

skills.name: Psionic Channeling
skills.definition: Psionic Channeling allows the user to act as a conduit for mental or psychic force. It governs how much psionic energy can be safely drawn, stored, or directed. Used with Psionic Meditation to determine energy recovery.

skills.name: Psionic Meditation
skills.definition: Psionic Meditation builds calm within the storm of thought. It enables the user to recover spent psionic energy and stabilize their internal field. Used to multiply psionic regeneration through mental clarity.

skills.name: Persuasion
skills.definition: The foundational skill for influencing thoughts, emotions, and behavior through charm, tone, and compelling language. Persuasion opens the door to cooperation without coercion, turning resistance into willingness and transforming interactions into opportunity.

skills.name: Negotiation
skills.definition: The practiced art of reaching mutual benefit through conversation, compromise, and strategy. Negotiation thrives in tense standoffs, business deals, and political meetings—where stakes are high and every word has weight.

skills.name: Win Win Bargaining
skills.definition: A diplomatic approach to deal-making that prioritizes shared benefit over dominance. This technique leaves both sides feeling heard, valued, and willing to engage again. Especially useful in alliances, trade, or team cohesion.

skills.name: Hard Ball Tactics
skills.definition: An aggressive form of negotiation focused on leverage, intimidation, or strategic threats. Ideal for pressing advantage or forcing concessions in cutthroat dealings, though often at the cost of goodwill.

skills.name: Concession Cue
skills.definition: The intuitive sense for when and how to offer just enough to break a stalemate without undermining your position. It’s the art of giving up little to gain much—often unlocking progress with a single, well-placed gesture.

skills.name: Diplomacy
skills.definition: The high art of peaceful engagement across differing interests, nations, or ideologies. Diplomacy defuses conflict, fosters alliances, and ensures that the wrong word doesn't start a war. It’s language as armor and olive branch.

skills.name: Protocol Fluency
skills.definition: Mastery of etiquette, cultural customs, and ceremonial nuance. This fluency earns respect in formal settings and prevents unintentional offenses that could derail progress or provoke retaliation.

skills.name: Crisis Mediation
skills.definition: The ability to maintain control, de-escalate tension, and restore communication during volatile or emotionally charged conflicts. It’s diplomacy under fire—when stakes are high and words must be chosen with care.

skills.name: Treaty Draft
skills.definition: The crafting of binding agreements with foresight, legal weight, and diplomatic finesse. Treaties shaped through this skill endure pressure, resist exploitation, and signal power through precision.

skills.name: Inspiration
skills.definition: The capacity to ignite passion, hope, or resolve in others through tone, presence, or message. Inspiration rallies hearts when logic fails and elevates morale when odds turn grim.

skills.name: Rally Speech
skills.definition: A powerful oration designed to unify, embolden, or galvanize a group. Whether before battle or at the brink of despair, the right words delivered at the right time can shift fate.

skills.name: Morale Boost
skills.definition: The steady practice of uplifting others through encouragement, empathy, and recognition. Morale Boost reinforces emotional resilience, especially during hardship, uncertainty, or long-term stress.

skills.name: Vision Casting
skills.definition: The ability to speak a dream so clearly that others begin to believe in it. Vision Casting turns abstract ideas into shared purpose, rallying followers to pursue goals that may not yet exist—but feel within reach.

skills.name: Deception
skills.definition: The core ability to obscure truth, manipulate perception, or create entirely false narratives. Deception doesn’t rely on brute strength or logic—it thrives on confidence, timing, and the subtle art of misdirection.

skills.name: Bluffing
skills.definition: The craft of projecting certainty behind a false claim. Bluffing works by making others question their instincts—whether at a poker table, during interrogation, or when caught in a lie.

skills.name: Quick Lie
skills.definition: The rapid delivery of a falsehood with no hesitation, often used to redirect suspicion or buy precious seconds. It's a survival mechanism for fast talkers under pressure.

skills.name: Plausible Detail
skills.definition: The strategic insertion of small, believable facts that make a lie harder to detect. These anchors ground fiction in just enough reality to disarm doubt.

skills.name: Confidence Ploy
skills.definition: The use of unshakable demeanor, commanding tone, and assertive posture to make a falsehood seem undeniable. Sometimes people don’t believe what’s said—they believe how it's said.

skills.name: Disguise
skills.definition: The act of altering physical appearance, voice, or demeanor to mask identity. Disguise isn't just makeup and costumes—it’s posture, habits, and the ability to vanish in plain sight.

skills.name: Costuming
skills.definition: Mastery over outfits, accessories, and visual cues that can establish new roles or backgrounds. From noble silks to peasant rags, each stitch sells the story.

skills.name: Voice Mimicry
skills.definition: The ability to replicate vocal tone, cadence, and mannerisms—useful for impersonation, infiltration, or sowing confusion. In the right hands, a single phrase can open locked doors.

skills.name: Identity Swap
skills.definition: The complete assumption of another persona, including their habits, gestures, and history. At this level, even close allies might be fooled… or replaced.

skills.name: Forgery
skills.definition: The creation or alteration of physical documents to simulate authenticity. From fake IDs to royal decrees, forgery opens doors and covers tracks.

skills.name: Signature Copy
skills.definition: Precise replication of handwriting, seals, or marks. Ideal for validating forgeries, falsifying orders, or stealing identity in ink.

skills.name: Document Aging
skills.definition: Techniques for making fake materials appear ancient, damaged, or timeworn. This adds a layer of realism that helps forgeries pass inspection or fool historians.

skills.name: Security Hologram
skills.definition: The reproduction of magical seals, digital signatures, or arcane emblems. Useful in high-tech or enchanted settings where illusion must match complexity.

skills.name: Performance Arts
skills.definition: The expressive mastery of emotion, rhythm, and presence across visual, vocal, and physical mediums. Whether to entertain, provoke, or inspire, performance transforms talent into influence and the mundane into magic.

skills.name: Acting
skills.definition: The ability to convincingly portray characters, personas, or emotional states not your own. Acting bridges illusion and reality—whether on stage or in subterfuge.

skills.name: Method Performance
skills.definition: Full emotional and physical immersion into a role, blurring the line between performer and character. This depth of embodiment creates performances that are hauntingly real.

skills.name: Improvised Scene
skills.definition: The spontaneous crafting of dialogue and action without script or planning. This skill shines under pressure—when only instinct and imagination remain.

skills.name: Stage Presence
skills.definition: The commanding aura that draws and holds attention effortlessly. A strong presence doesn’t beg to be seen—it demands it with posture, pause, and poise.

skills.name: Music
skills.definition: The emotional manipulation of sound through melody, rhythm, and harmony. Music bypasses thought to strike the heart directly—whether through joy, sorrow, or awe.

skills.name: Instrument Mastery
skills.definition: Technical fluency and expressive control over one or more instruments. Mastery enables improvisation, resilience under pressure, and deep emotional resonance.

skills.name: Vocal Control
skills.definition: The precise shaping of breath, tone, and inflection across spoken and sung voice. Whether enchanting an audience or commanding a room, voice becomes a weapon and a brushstroke.

skills.name: Crowd Sync
skills.definition: The ability to sense and ride the emotional wave of an audience, adjusting tempo and delivery to amplify connection. When performed properly, the crowd moves as one.

skills.name: Storytelling
skills.definition: The art of delivering tales that inform, inspire, or deceive. Storytelling transfers culture, binds groups, and makes abstract truths personal through character and conflict.

skills.name: Narrative Weaving
skills.definition: The layering of subtext, theme, and subtle cues into stories that operate on multiple levels. The listener leaves entertained—but also changed.

skills.name: Dramatic Timing
skills.definition: The mastery of pauses, reveals, and escalation that give words their punch. Whether invoking laughter, dread, or silence, this skill controls emotional rhythm.

skills.name: Audience Hook
skills.definition: The technique of gripping attention from the very first sentence. Hooks plant curiosity, emotional investment, or surprise, ensuring the listener can’t look away.

skills.name: Leadership
skills.definition: The ability to guide, unite, and empower others toward a shared objective. Leadership transforms will into motion and chaos into structure. It is not about control—it’s about conviction, presence, and the ability to inspire trust.

skills.name: Command
skills.definition: The executional side of leadership—delivering decisive orders that maintain discipline and direction. Command thrives in urgent moments when hesitation risks collapse.

skills.name: Clear Orders
skills.definition: The skill of issuing short, specific, and instantly understood directives. A well-placed command can cut through noise, panic, or indecision with surgical precision.

skills.name: Crisis Control
skills.definition: The ability to impose calm and clarity during chaos. Crisis Control is what separates a true leader from a loud voice—guiding others when plans fall apart.

skills.name: Chain of Command
skills.definition: The establishment and reinforcement of role hierarchy within a group. This ensures efficiency, accountability, and the smooth handoff of power under stress.

skills.name: Strategic Communication
skills.definition: The deliberate use of words, tone, and timing to deliver critical information that aligns goals and minimizes confusion. It’s not what you say—it’s when, how, and to whom.

skills.name: Briefing Clarity
skills.definition: The ability to deliver mission objectives, roles, and contingencies in ways that are actionable and unforgettable. Everyone leaves the room knowing exactly what they must do.

skills.name: Rally Pointing
skills.definition: The verbal or symbolic act of refocusing scattered teams under stress. Whether calling a charge or issuing a fallback, this skill restores cohesion and momentum.

skills.name: Dynamic Re Tasking
skills.definition: The flexible redirection of team roles or objectives mid-operation. A leader with this skill doesn’t panic when the plan breaks—they adapt without losing unity.

skills.name: Mentorship
skills.definition: The long-form cultivation of others’ growth, skill, and character. Mentorship is the slow fire that forges future leaders, craftsmen, or heroes through presence and patience.

skills.name: Skill Coaching
skills.definition: The focused refinement of a specific talent through guidance, demonstration, and feedback. This builds both competence and confidence.

skills.name: Morale Nurturing
skills.definition: The steady reinforcement of emotional stability, encouragement, and purpose—especially during long campaigns or personal struggle.

skills.name: Talent Cultivation
skills.definition: The act of spotting hidden potential and creating space for it to thrive. This skill sees the greatness in others before they do—and helps bring it to the surface.

skills.name: Streetwise
skills.definition: The instinctive social navigation of gritty, unpredictable, or illicit environments. Streetwise isn’t book smart—it’s survival smart, built from hard knocks, fast talk, and watching your back.

skills.name: Underworld Contacts
skills.definition: The cultivation of useful relationships in the criminal, fringe, or gray zones of society. These aren’t friends—they’re assets, each with a price and a pulse on what’s hidden.

skills.name: Informant Network
skills.definition: A web of eyes and ears across streets, slums, ports, or dens—each passing along information for coin or favor. When something happens in the shadows, you hear it first.

skills.name: Favour Trade
skills.definition: The subtle bartering of services, secrets, and leverage instead of coin. This is the economy of trust and threat—where reputation buys more than gold.

skills.name: Safe House Locator
skills.definition: The ability to identify, access, or maintain hidden bolt-holes and neutral shelters across cities. Knowing where to vanish—or stash others—can mean survival or silence.

skills.name: Black-Market Trade
skills.definition: A savvy grasp of illegal, restricted, or underground commerce. From weapons to rare ingredients, this skill knows where to find it, who’s selling, and how to avoid getting burned.

skills.name: Hot Goods Appraise
skills.definition: The ability to quickly assess stolen, counterfeit, or smuggled items for value, origin, and risk. It’s part fence, part forensics, part street intuition.

skills.name: Off Book Deal
skills.definition: Negotiating transactions that leave no trace—no witnesses, no ledgers, no strings. These deals operate outside law, guild, or faction, but not without danger.

skills.name: Smuggling Route
skills.definition: Knowledge of hidden tunnels, bribed officials, and detour paths to move goods unseen. This skill is both a mental map and a rolodex of risky partners.

skills.name: Urban Survival (Social)
skills.definition: The day-to-day etiquette, slang, and posture needed to avoid standing out—or offending the wrong group—in densely populated or hostile zones. It’s camouflage through behavior.

skills.name: Local Slang
skills.definition: The vocabulary, tone, and rhythm of regional or criminal dialects. Speaking the local code keeps you inside the tribe—and outside suspicion.

skills.name: Turf Etiquette
skills.definition: Knowing which streets are controlled by whom, what colors not to wear, and when to speak or stay silent. This prevents casual mistakes from becoming fatal ones.

skills.name: Exit Sense
skills.definition: A sixth sense for escape routes, choke points, or blind spots in any urban sprawl. It’s the instinct that kicks in before things go loud.

skills.name: Intimidating Presence
skills.definition: The ability to command space, silence rooms, or shake confidence without lifting a finger. This isn’t about shouting or violence—it’s the raw, unspoken sense that you are not to be crossed. It simmers beneath words, carved in posture and gaze.

skills.name: Menacing Glare
skills.definition: The use of intense eye contact to unsettle, dominate, or provoke hesitation. It communicates threat without noise—just pressure, like a predator sizing up prey.

skills.name: Predatory Stare
skills.definition: A gaze that instills primal fear, as if you already know how they’ll fall. It pierces false bravado and awakens instinctive dread in the weak-willed.

skills.name: Fear Spike
skills.definition: The sudden projection of hostility or intent that sends adrenaline surging through the target. A fear spike is fast, sharp, and destabilizing—designed to make them flinch first.

skills.name: Resolve Drain
skills.definition: A sustained, silent stare that chips away at inner fortitude. Confidence erodes with each passing second under your eyes, until even the bold begin to waver.

skills.name: Dominant Stance
skills.definition: The calculated use of posture, height, and spatial control to assert physical and psychological superiority. It tells the room who’s in charge without needing a word.

skills.name: Alpha Presence
skills.definition: A fully embodied aura of control that suppresses challenge and invites compliance. Others feel compelled to follow, defer, or step aside, often without knowing why.

skills.name: Space Invade
skills.definition: The deliberate violation of another’s physical boundary to provoke fear, discomfort, or retreat. Used precisely, it destabilizes even confident opponents.

skills.name: Commanding Posture
skills.definition: A physically grounded stance that projects unwavering authority. It doesn’t demand attention—it makes others feel off-balance if they don’t give it.

skills.name: Threat Projection
skills.definition: The performative expression of danger—through tone, implication, or gesture. This skill doesn’t promise violence directly, but it lets everyone know it’s on the table.

skills.name: Verbal Menace
skills.definition: The calculated use of tone, pacing, and implication to make a threat hit deeper than the words alone. A good verbal menace leaves targets questioning what’s real and what’s bluff.

skills.name: Weapon Display
skills.definition: The subtle—or overt—presentation of arms to send a message. It’s not drawn to attack; it’s revealed to remind. When done right, no blood need spill.

skills.name: Psychological Edge
skills.definition: The layering of mind games, veiled threats, and strategic pressure to keep opponents off balance. It’s not about overpowering—it’s about making them second-guess every move.

skills.name: Networking
skills.definition: The practiced ability to form lasting, meaningful connections across varied circles. Networking isn't just about shaking hands—it's about positioning yourself as a presence people remember, respect, and invite into opportunity. Relationships become leverage, and conversations become doors.

skills.name: Influence Networks
skills.definition: A cultivated web of allies, advocates, and gatekeepers capable of shaping perception or opening unseen pathways. These networks span factions, guilds, and social layers—quietly pulling strings behind polite smiles.

skills.name: Public Impact Timing
skills.definition: The ability to read the moment and strike socially when it will echo the loudest. Whether launching an idea or exposing a truth, this skill makes sure eyes and ears are waiting.

skills.name: Message Targeting
skills.definition: The refinement of message, tone, and method to fit the intended audience with surgical precision. The same truth lands very differently when spoken in the right dialect, at the right table.

skills.name: Message Control
skills.definition: The ability to guide what spreads and what stalls—through silence, redirection, or subtle reinforcement. It’s not about censorship; it’s about social engineering, quietly sculpting public memory.

skills.name: Professional Circles
skills.definition: The art of moving within formal hierarchies—guilds, orders, firms, or factions—without losing individuality. This skill opens closed doors through recognition, merit, and mutual gain.

skills.name: Elevator Pitch
